description_long: This is a rectangular brick factory building with a gabled slate roof and a tall cylindrical smokestack rising from one side. The facade features large arched industrial windows with black metal frames, providing ample natural light to the interior. The brickwork is detailed, with decorative parapets and pilasters at the corners, and the roof is punctuated by several long, narrow skylights. The front of the building has a large arched entryway flanked by two circular windows, typical of early 20th-century industrial architecture. The structure appears robust and utilitarian, designed for heavy industrial use, with no visible rooftop machinery or loading dock doors. The overall appearance is that of a historic factory, likely used for manufacturing or warehousing in an urban setting.
description: A historic brick factory building with a gabled slate roof, tall smokestack, large arched industrial windows, and decorative brickwork. The structure is robust and utilitarian, typical of early 20th-century industrial architecture.
description_view_0: The main facade shows three large arched windows, a single arched entryway, and the base of the tall smokestack on the left.
description_view_1: The gabled end features a large arched entryway and two circular windows above, with the smokestack rising behind.
description_view_2: The opposite long side displays three large arched windows and the smokestack emerging from the roof, with no entryway visible.
description_view_3: The rear gabled end is plain brick with no windows or doors, and the smokestack is prominent on the left.
